'''Thomas Opacic''' ({{IPAc-en|oʊ|p|ə|tʃ|ɪ|k}}) (born 7 September 1994) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] in the [[Hostplus Cup]].

He previously played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]], [[North Queensland Cowboys]], and the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Opacic was born in [[Redcliffe, Queensland]], [[Australia]], and is of [[Serbians|Serbian]] descent.<ref name="couriermail1">{{cite news|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/brisbane-broncos-rookie-tom-opacic-wanted-by-serbia-for-2017-rugby-league-world-cup-bid/news-story/85bd8c57ee66fb08597f5856a407b3b7|title=Brisbane Broncos rookie Tom Opacic wanted by Serbia for 2017 Rugby League World Cup bid|newspaper=The Courier Mail|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|date=2015-05-02|access-date=2016-06-25|last1=Badel|first1=Peter}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
[[File:Tom Opacic.jpg |Opacic playing for the Broncos in 2014|thumb|right]]
In 2013 and 2014, Opacic played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/o/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150616154246/http://nycdatabase.org/players/o/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=16 June 2015|title=O|date=27 December 2012|publisher=Nyc Database|access-date=2016-06-25}}</ref> before graduating to their [[Queensland Cup]] team, and his old junior club, Redcliffe Dolphins in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2015/03/03/intrust_super_cup_rd.html|title=Intrust Super Cup Rd 1 teams|date=3 March 2015|publisher=QRL|access-date=2016-06-25}}</ref>

===2016===
In Round 16 of the [[2016 NRL season]], Opacic made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Brisbane club against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-bulldogs-v-broncos/tabid/10874/newsid/98480/default.aspx|title=Updated team lists: Bulldogs v Broncos|publisher=NRL.com|date=2016-02-28|access-date=2016-06-25}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.broncos.com.au/news/2016/06/25/late_mail_broncos_v_.af_news.html|title=Late Mail - Broncos v Bulldogs|publisher=Broncos|access-date=2016-06-25}}</ref> In September, he re-signed with Brisbane on a two-year contract until the end of 2018.<ref>{{cite web|author=Broncos.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/broncos-re-sign-nrl-rookies-23767/|title=Broncos re-sign NRL rookies|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2016-08-26|access-date=2016-09-15}}</ref>

===2017===
Opacic failed to play a first grade game in 2017, after undergoing two shoulder reconstructions in the summer to treat injuries he suffered in the 2016 finals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/brisbane-gun-to-miss-2017-nrl-season-start|title = Brisbane gun to miss 2017 NRL season start}}</ref> During Opacic's recovery, [[Tautau Moga]] became one of the first choice centres for Brisbane that year, relegating him to the Queensland Cup that season.{{citation needed|date=May 2023}}

===2018===
The signing of [[Jack Bird]], as well as the emergence of promising outside backs, [[Kotoni Staggs]] and [[Jamayne Isaako]], meant that Opacic spent most of 2018 playing for the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] in the Queensland Cup competition. He scored his only try for the season, coming off the bench in Brisbane's 24-20 win against their rivals, the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], in Round 2.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/nrl-brisbane-broncos-vs-north-queensland-cowboys-round-2/9659b59e-6440-45c5-82b5-c5b4d84bc312|title = NRL highlights, result: Broncos vs Cowboys| date=16 March 2018 }}</ref> Opacic appeared in 10 games for the Brisbane club in the 2018 season, starting in six and coming off the bench in four.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MS0xNzE3LTQtLS0tLS0yMjc4NC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title = Custom Match List - Rugby League Project}}</ref>

On 8 August, Opacic re-signed with the Brisbane side on a two-year deal but a month later was released by the club to sign with the North Queensland Cowboys until the end of 2020.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/01/bennett-reveals-why-broncos-agreed-to-release-tom-opacic-to-cowboysf/|title=Why Broncos agreed to release Opacic to Cowboys|date=September 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/10/11/four-more-signings-for-cowboys/|title = Four more signings for Cowboys|date = 11 October 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
In Round 1 of the [[2019 NRL season]], Opacic made his club debut for North Queensland in their 24–12 win over the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/03/12/nrl-team-list-round-1-v-dragons/|title=NRL team list: Round 1 v Dragons|date=12 March 2019}}</ref> Following a Round 2 defeat by Brisbane, Opacic was dropped to the [[Townsville Blackhawks]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/111737156/league-player-knocked-out-by-exnrl-hooker-blames-king-hit-on-himself|title = League player knocked out by ex-NRL hooker blames king hit on himself|date = 2 April 2019}}</ref> Opacic returned to first grade in North Queensland's Round 8 win over the [[Gold Coast Titans]]. A week later, he scored his first try for the club in a 16–32 loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-12/rabbits-down-the-cowboys-roosters-win-to-end-magic-round/11103988|title = Rabbits down the Cowboys, Roosters win to end Magic Round|newspaper = ABC News|date = 12 May 2019}}</ref>

After his early struggles, Opacic locked down the right centre position for the North Queensland club, playing the remaining 17 games, scoring seven tries and forming a dynamic partnership with winger [[Kyle Feldt]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboys/offfield-friendship-is-taking-over-the-cowboys-tryscoring-ranks/news-story/290d3e1a5df45f7a9be0f4d017e5b327|title = Subscribe to the Townsville Bulletin}}</ref>

===2020===
In February, Opacic was a member of the Cowboys' [[2020 NRL Nines]] winning squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/02/15/nines-final-team-list-final-v-dragons/|title=Nines final team list: Final v Dragons|date=15 February 2020}}</ref> Opacic played just five NRL games in 2020, scoring two tries, struggling to hold down a starting {{rlp|ce}} position. On 3 October, the North Queensland club announced that Opacic would be departing the club at the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/10/03/cowboys-to-farewell-five-departing-players/|title=Cowboys to farewell five departing players|date=3 October 2020|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref> On 21 October, it was announced that Opacic had signed a one-year deal with the [[Parramatta Eels]].

===2021===
He made his debut for Parramatta in round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]] where the club recorded a 24-16 victory over Brisbane.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-03-12/brisbane-broncos-lose-to-parramatta-eels-xavier-coates-injured/13242554/|title=Brisbane Broncos' NRL losing streak continues as Parramatta Eels score comeback 24-16 win; Newcastle Knights beat Bulldogs|newspaper=ABC News|date=12 March 2021}}</ref>

In round 4, he scored two tries for Parramatta in a 36-22 victory over the [[Wests Tigers]] at [[Stadium Australia]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-04-05/nrl-scorecentre-updates-wests-tigers-parramatta-eels/100046150/|title=Wests Tigers 22 Parramatta 36|newspaper=ABC News|date=5 April 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
Opacic started the season with Parramatta's [[NSW Cup]] team and scored four tries in the clubs 34-6 opening round victory over [[St. George Illawarra|St. George]].  Following an injury to [[Sean Russell (rugby league)|Sean Russell]], Opacic was called into the NRL squad for the club's round 2 match against Cronulla.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-reserve-grade-wrap-new-south-wales-cup-tariq-sims-george-burgess-dragons-tom-opacic-eels-shaquai-mitchell-rabbitohs/news-story/ad32da671552353fd224bbad775cb25b/|title=Solution to Eels' injury crisis emerges; Raiders young gun puts pressure on Ricky: Reserves Wrap|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=15 March 2022 }}</ref>
On 10 August, Opacic signed a two-year deal to join English side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] starting in 2023.
In round 24, Opacic scored two tries for Parramatta in a 53-6 victory over [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].  Opacic played in Parramatta's opening two finals matches against Penrith and Canberra but suffered a hamstring injury during the clubs win over the latter opponent.  Opacic subsequently missed Parramatta's upset preliminary final victory over [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] and the [[2022 NRL Grand Final]] against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] which Parramatta lost 28-12.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-brisbane-broncos-vs-parramatta-eels-live-score-updates-teams-start-time-supercoach-scores-video/news-story/6d4dc68c74a920399c26ea8f2614fdda/|title=Broncos' season 'on life support' after horror 113pt fortnight as Eels 'weapon' runs riot: Big hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=25 August 2022 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12669275/tom-opacic-joins-hull-kr-from-parramatta-eels-for-2023/|title=Tom Opacic joins Hull KR from Parramatta Eels for 2023|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/we-hate-parra-panther-mocks-eels-as-koroisau-pokes-fun-at-tigers-20221003-p5bmr0.html/|title=We hate Parra as Panthers mock Eels as Koroisau pokes fun at Tigers|website=www.smh.com.au|date=3 October 2022 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2022/oct/02/nrl-grand-final-2022-live-penrith-panthers-vs-parramatta-eels-scores-winners-win-team-news-lists-kickoff-start-time-game-score-updates-teams|title=NRL Grand Final as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2023===
Opacic made his club debut for Hull Kingston Rovers in round 1 of the 2023 Super League season as they upset [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] 27-18.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668447|title=Hull KR 27-18 Wigan: Shaun Kenny-Dowall scores first Super League hat-trick in Rovers win|work=BBC Sport |date=18 February 2023 }}</ref>
On 12 August, Opacic played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport |date=12 August 2023 }}</ref>
Opacic played 26 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
On 19 August, Hull KR confirmed that Opacic would depart the team at the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-08-19 |title=Tom Opacic to depart at the end of the 2024 season |url=https://hullkr.co.uk/news/tom-opacic-to-depart-at-the-end-of-the-2024-season |access-date=2024-12-21 |website=hullkr.co.uk |language=en}}</ref> On 20 December, Opacic signed with the Redcliffe Dolphins.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2024-12-20 |title=Ex-NRL centre signs with Australian team |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/ex-nrl-centre-signs-with-australian-team-217766/ |access-date=2024-12-21 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>

==Achievements and accolades==
===Team===
*'''[[2020 NRL Nines]]''': North Queensland Cowboys – Winners
*'''[[2023 Challenge Cup]]''': Hull Kingston Rovers – Runners-Up
*'''[[2024 Super League Grand Final]]''': Hull Kingston Rovers – Runners-Up
